name: lbfgsb_max_climb_raw_jitter
description: Direct per-frame L-BFGS-B solution for maximum steady-state climb rate. This is a research result, not a smooth player-friendly strategy.
angleConvention: positive angle means nose-up; negative angle means nose-down
notes: This solution beats the smoother segmented climb-rate strategy in climb rate, but it contains severe frame-to-frame pitch oscillation.
The high-frequency segment alternates near the pitch bounds, with RMS angle delta about 36.16 degrees/tick and max delta 90 degrees/tick.
It is useful as an optimization reference and as evidence that unconstrained per-frame control can exploit very jittery policies.
